votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if the boolean result matches the right-angle triangle condition.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if the boolean result matches the condition for a right-angled triangle.
The Python code correctly checks if 'res' matches the boolean evaluation of the Pythagorean theorem conditions, which is exactly what the Coq specification requires.

The Python code correctly checks whether res equals the boolean result of the right-angle triangle condition. The Coq spec states res=true iff one of the three Pythagorean conditions holds, which is equivalent to res being equal to the boolean value of that disjunction. For concrete inputs, both will agree on whether res correctly indicates a right-angle triangle.

The Python code correctly checks if res equals the boolean indicating whether any permutation of the sides satisfies the Pythagorean theorem. The Coq spec states res=true iff one of the three conditions holds, which is equivalent to res being equal to the disjunction of those conditions. Python's boolean equality check produces the same concrete result.

The Python code correctly checks whether res equals the boolean indicating if any of the three Pythagorean conditions hold. The Coq spec states res=true iff one of the three squared-sum equalities holds, which is exactly what the Python code computes: res should be True when is_right_angle is True, and False otherwise. The biconditional in Coq (res = true <-> condition) means res is true exactly when the condition holds, which matches the Python equality check res == is_right_angle.
Both define res to be true exactly when any Pythagorean equality among a, b, c holds; Python returns res == computed condition, matching the Coq iff specification on all integer inputs.
Both check Pythagorean equalities on arbitrary integers and return true exactly when res matches that condition; Python’s **2 matches Coq’s ^2 on Z.
Python computes the Pythagorean condition in all three permutations and returns res == condition, which matches the Coq spec res = true <-> (Pythagorean condition). Both use exact integer arithmetic.
